# PumpKit

> Open-source TypeScript framework for building PumpFun Telegram bots on Solana.

PumpKit is a monorepo of production-ready Telegram bots for monitoring PumpFun activity on Solana. It provides shared building blocks (bot scaffolding, Solana monitoring, formatters, storage, health checks) so you can ship a bot in hours, not weeks.

## Packages

- @pumpkit/core — Shared framework: bot scaffolding, Solana monitoring, formatters, storage, config, health checks
- @pumpkit/monitor — All-in-one monitoring bot (fee claims, launches, graduations, whale trades, CTO alerts, REST API + SSE)
- @pumpkit/tracker — Group call-tracking bot (leaderboards, PNL cards, rankings, multi-chain, SQLite)
- @pumpkit/channel — Read-only Telegram channel feed (5 event types, HTML messages)
- @pumpkit/claim — Fee claim tracker (by token CA or X handle, Twitter follower display)
- @pumpkit/web — Frontend dashboard (coming soon)

## Tech Stack

- TypeScript (ES2022, strict mode, ES modules)
- grammy (Telegram bot framework)
- @solana/web3.js (Solana RPC)
- better-sqlite3 (tracker database)
- canvas (PNL card generation)
- Turborepo (monorepo orchestration)
- Docker + Railway (deployment)

## On-Chain Programs Monitored

- Pump: 6EF8rrecthR5Dkzon8Nwu78hRvfCKubJ14M5uBEwF6P — Bonding curve operations
- PumpAMM: pAMMBay6oceH9fJKBRHGP5D4bD4sWpmSwMn52FMfXEA — Graduated AMM pools
- PumpFees: pfeeUxB6jkeY1Hxd7CsFCAjcbHA9rWtchMGdZ6VojVZ — Fee sharing

## Quick Start

```bash
git clone https://github.com/nirholas/pumpkit.git
cd pumpkit && npm install
cp packages/monitor/.env.example packages/monitor/.env
npm run dev --workspace=@pumpkit/monitor
```

## npm Status

Not yet published. Use monorepo workspace references for now. See docs/npm.md for roadmap.

- Repository: https://github.com/nirholas/pumpkit
